question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker whose offer array contains a single sell offer with a tick near MAX_TICK (e.g., tick=5820) such that TickLib.tickToPrice(tick) + settlementFee > WAD trigger TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits to revert with PriceGreaterThanOne() outside the try/catch block, blocking the entire bundle even though the offer is otherwise valid and the taker is authorized? Preconditions: market created, taker authorized, offer.buy==false, tick chosen so buyerPrice > WAD at current TTM. Call sequence: taker calls buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral -> touchMarket -> pullToken (succeeds) -> forceApproveMax -> loop iteration i=0 -> TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits reverts with PriceGreaterThanOne() (uncaught). Invariant tested: valid taker actions must not be permanently blocked by uncaught library reverts on individual offers. Scoped impact: fund freeze — taker cannot execute any bundle buy even with valid offers in the same array. Proof idea: fuzz tick values near MAX_TICK and settlementFee breakpoints; assert that buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral reverts for any offer where tickToPrice(tick) + settlementFee > WAD, even when other offers in the array are valid.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker whose offer array contains a buy offer where offerPrice < settlementFee (realistic near-maturity condition) trigger TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits to revert with an underflow outside the try/catch block, blocking the entire bundle including valid offers at other indices? Preconditions: market near maturity (TTM < 1 day), buy offer with tick such that tickToPrice(tick) < settlementFee(id, TTM), taker authorized. Call sequence: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget -> touchMarket -> collateral supply loop -> take loop i=0 -> TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits called (uncaught) -> reverts. Invariant tested: uncaught library reverts on individual offers must not block the entire bundle. Scoped impact: fund freeze — seller cannot execute bundle sell near maturity. Proof idea: fuzz TTM in [0, 86400] and offer tick; assert revert occurs when tickToPrice(tick) < settlementFee and confirm no try/catch wraps the TakeAmountsLib call.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker pass an empty takes[] array causing an array-out-of-bounds panic at takes[0].offer.market.loanToken (line 62) before any authorization or permit check, permanently blocking the call? Invariant tested: bundle entry points must not panic on empty input arrays; they should revert with a descriptive error. Scoped impact: fund freeze — any caller passing empty takes[] gets an opaque panic revert. Proof idea: unit test with takes.length == 0; assert revert type is panic(0x32) (array out of bounds) rather than a named error.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker pass an empty takes[] array causing an array-out-of-bounds panic at takes[0].offer.market.loanToken (line 129) after collateral has already been supplied to Midnight, permanently blocking the sell leg? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker whose takes[] array contains a mix of valid sell offers and one offer with offer.buy==true at index i>0 trigger InconsistentSide() revert after some takes have already been attempted, blocking the entire bundle even though the remaining offers are valid? Preconditions: takes[0].offer.buy==false (valid), takes[1].offer.buy==true (wrong side), taker authorized. Call sequence: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral -> loop i=0 (take attempted via try/catch) -> loop i=1 -> require(!takes[1].offer.buy, InconsistentSide()) reverts (uncaught). Scoped impact: fund freeze — taker cannot complete a valid multi-offer bundle if any offer has wrong side flag. Proof idea: fuzz offer.buy flags across takes[]; assert InconsistentSide() reverts the entire tx for any mixed array.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker whose takes[] array contains offers from two different markets trigger InconsistentMarket() revert at index i>0, blocking the entire bundle even though takes[0] was valid and already attempted? Call sequence: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral -> id = toId(takes[0].offer.market) -> loop i=0 (take attempted) -> loop i=1 -> toId(takes[1].offer.market) != id -> InconsistentMarket() reverts. [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker whose offer has maxUnits==0 and maxAssets==0 cause ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits to return 0, making unitsToTake==0, causing take(offer, data, 0, ...) to be called, which if Midnight reverts on 0 units is caught but filledUnits never advances, ultimately triggering OutOfOffers() even though the taker is authorized and has sufficient collateral? Preconditions: offer.maxUnits==0, offer.maxAssets==0, taker authorized, targetUnits > 0. Call sequence: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral -> consumableUnits returns 0 -> min(remaining, takes[i].units, 0) == 0 -> take(offer, data, 0, ...) called -> revert caught or no-op -> filledUnits unchanged -> OutOfOffers(). Proof idea: fuzz offer.maxUnits and offer.maxAssets at 0; assert OutOfOffers() is thrown.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker trigger an exact-equality revert (filledBuyerAssets == targetFilledBuyerAssets fails) due to a 1-wei rounding discrepancy between TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits (which computes units to yield exactly targetBuyerAssets) and Midnight's actual take() rounding, blocking a valid buy even though all offers are valid and the taker is authorized? Preconditions: taker authorized, offer valid, targetFilledBuyerAssets chosen such that buyerAssetsToUnits returns N units but take(N) yields targetFilledBuyerAssets ± 1 due to mulDivDown/Up rounding differences. Call sequence: buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral -> TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits(targetFilledBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets) -> take(N) -> resBuyerAssets = targetFilledBuyerAssets ± 1 -> filledBuyerAssets != targetFilledBuyerAssets -> OutOfOffers(). Invariant tested: rounding in TakeAmountsLib must be consistent with Midnight's take() rounding to avoid spurious OutOfOffers(). Scoped impact: fund freeze — taker cannot complete asset-targeted buy due to 1-wei rounding mismatch. Proof idea: differential fuzz test comparing TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits output against actual take() return value across all tick/fee/amount combinations.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker trigger an exact-equality revert (filledSellerAssets == targetFilledSellerAssets fails) due to a 1-wei rounding discrepancy between TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits and Midnight's actual take() rounding, blocking a valid sell even though all offers are valid? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker trigger an arithmetic underflow revert on the refund line (maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets) when referralFeeAssets = filledBuyerAssets * referralFeePct / (WAD - referralFeePct) exceeds maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets, blocking a valid buy even though all takes succeeded? Call sequence: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral -> all takes succeed -> filledUnits == targetUnits -> referralFeeAssets = filledBuyerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD - referralFeePct) > maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets -> underflow revert on line 104. Scoped impact: fund freeze — taker cannot complete bundle buy when referral fee exceeds remaining maxBuyerAssets budget. Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct in [0, WAD-1] and filledBuyerAssets; assert underflow condition and check if maxBuyerAssets constraint is sufficient.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower trigger a revert in the withdrawCollateral loop (e.g., due to oracle price drop between repay and withdrawal making the health check fail) that reverts the entire tx including the repay, blocking a valid repay-and-withdraw action even though the position was healthy before the call? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker using a fee-on-transfer loan token trigger OutOfOffers() because pullToken pulls maxBuyerAssets but the bundler receives maxBuyerAssets * (1 - fee_rate), causing all take() calls to fail (insufficient balance) with reverts caught by try/catch, so filledUnits never advances?
